highlights.text: I wrote last Monday at 4pm (when we were 6340s): “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.

The trap was in and we went parabolic after this, I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm after this back-test was complete that: “The bull case though is that ES can fill it out the upside and test 6360, 6366, then 6371-74.

A move like we saw last week takes time to digest and I wrote on Friday that: “My general lean if I had to give one though is that ES can cooloff now and do some backtesting and work on structure.” and “After a move like this, ES often needs to digest the move.” We spent today consolidating, resting, and grinding lower.
To do that, we need a setup, and I trade 1: The Failed Breakdown.
The Failed Breakdown is how institutions accumulate; they flush lows, trap shorts, and price rallies.
When you marry the Failed Breakdown, with macro chart patterns, magic happens, and we saw that this week.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a couple quality Failed Breakdowns we had on Tuesday and Wednesday last week which helped propel last weeks rally.

post_title: SPX Puts In Its First Dip Since July 15th. Will It Get Bought? July 29 Plan
